report_name: Europol IOCTA Report: AI, Encryption, and Data Theft are Fueling an Industrialized Cybercrime Wave

threat_actors.name: Cybercriminal Groups
threat_actors.description: Financially motivated cybercriminal groups that are increasingly shifting to data theft and extortion, leveraging as-a-service models and advanced tools like AI. The IOCTA report notes over 120 active ransomware brands were observed in 2025.

threat_actors.name: State-sponsored Actors
threat_actors.description: Nation-state actors who are increasingly using cybercriminal groups as proxies to conduct disruptive attacks and achieve geopolitical objectives, blurring the line with financially motivated crime.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used by cybercriminals to gain initial access. The report highlights that Generative AI is used to create more convincing, personalized, and grammatically correct phishing emails and to automate social engineering, including the use of deepfake audio/video.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Used as part of a multi-faceted extortion strategy by ransomware groups. However, the report notes a strategic shift away from pure encryption towards data theft as the primary leverage.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: A primary tactic for modern ransomware groups, who prioritize stealing sensitive data from victim organizations and then threatening to leak it publicly as part of their extortion strategy.

attack_patterns.name: Financial Theft
attack_patterns.description: The primary goal of financially motivated cybercriminals, achieved through ransomware and data extortion, with proceeds laundered through cryptocurrency mixers and privacy coins.

attack_patterns.name: Proxy
attack_patterns.description: Used by cybercriminals to obfuscate their location and activities, contributing to their anonymity and making attribution by law enforcement more difficult.

attack_patterns.name: Encrypted Channel
attack_patterns.description: Used for command and control communications, with attackers abusing end-to-end encrypted platforms to evade detection and monitoring.

attack_patterns.name: Develop Capabilities
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use Generative AI to assist less-skilled actors in developing malicious code and finding vulnerabilities, contributing to the industrialization of cybercrime.

tools.name: Generative AI
tools.description: Legitimate Generative AI models are abused by attackers to scale attacks, create convincing phishing lures, automate social engineering, and generate malicious code.

tools.name: Cryptocurrency Mixers
tools.description: Used by cybercriminals to launder illicit proceeds from their attacks, making it difficult for law enforcement to trace funds.

infrastructure.name: Bulletproof Hosting Services
infrastructure.description: Used by criminals for obfuscation and to host malicious infrastructure, making it resilient to takedowns.

infrastructure.name: Proxy Services
infrastructure.description: Used by cybercriminals to hide their true location and anonymize their attack traffic.

infrastructure.name: Decentralized Cryptocurrency Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Includes decentralized cryptocurrency mixers and privacy coins used to launder money from cybercriminal operations.
